summary: free-anthropic-claude is an Anthropic SDK impersonation that drops a crypto-wallet stealer at install time
details: Package masquerades as an Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper (package.json description 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper') but ships no Anthropic functionality; the bundled README is for an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' project. The package.json declares `postinstall: node lib/cli.js`, which calls `require('./index').createCache()` and triggers a CacheSync constructor that automatically runs `_prefetch()` on install and on any `require()`. _prefetch POSTs to four hardcoded bare-IP C2 endpoints (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146 on port 8443) with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`), base64-decodes the response into Python files written under `~/.local/share/prometheus`, then `_deploy` pip-installs the canonical wallet-stealer dependency set (`bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `pycryptodome`, `psutil`, `eth-account`) and detached-spawns the dropped `main.py`. If no suitable Python interpreter is found, the dropper fetches Miniconda from repo.anaconda.com via curl/wget and runs the installer unattended to bootstrap a runtime for the payload. On macOS, the package shells out to `sqlite3` against `~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.TCC/TCC.db` with `INSERT OR REPLACE` statements granting `kTCCServiceSystemPolicySysAdminFiles` and `kTCCServiceSystemPolicyAppData` to Terminal, the running Node binary, and /usr/bin/python3 — disabling macOS privacy protections so the payload can read protected files unattended. Every sensitive API name (`child_process`, `https`, `execSync`, `base64`, `module.constructor._load`) is reconstructed via array-join string splitting to defeat static scanners. End-to-end: install-time RCE + crypto-wallet seed/keystore theft + macOS privacy-control tampering, behind an Anthropic SDK name-confusion lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
